title: Miconia suprabasalis
intro: Miconia suprabasalis, also known as the "Brazilian cherry," is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions like wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Miconia suprabasalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Miconia suprabasalis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Miconia suprabasalis is classified in the following way: It belongs to the family Melastomataceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. Within this family, Miconia suprabasalis is further classified as a member of the genus Miconia, a large and diverse group of plants that are found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The genus Miconia is itself part of the order Myrtales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants with similar characteristics. The order Myrtales is then part of the clade Rosidae, a large and ancient group of flowering plants that are thought to have originated over 100 million years ago. Overall, Miconia suprabasalis is a member of the angiosperm clade, a group of flowering plants that are characterized by the production of flowers and seeds.
